page: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture operating at 3.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage consists of a 475 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data places this instance in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core Geekbench score of 1624.0 and a multi-core score of 9759.0, while the Passmark CPU score is 20852.39. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 834.81 GB/sec, which drops to 98.18 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 53.57 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance handles 15.66 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 7637.85 on Passmark database operations. Large language model inference benchmarks indicate a tradeoff: while prompt processing for a small 135M model reaches 1027.73 tokens/sec, prompt processing for a medium 7B model drops to a weak tier at 31.41 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x2iedn.4xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32:1, making it a specialized resource for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy tasks. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and dedicated CPU allocation supports workloads requiring high memory capacity alongside fast local scratch space. This server is configured for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and data processing tasks that fit within its 512.0 GB RAM footprint.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core, alongside a 475 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results show average tier performance, including a Passmark CPU score of 20852.39 and 15.66 million Redis SET operations per second. However, large language model prompt processing drops to a weak tier of 31.41 tokens/sec for 7B models. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iedn.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 475 GB NVMe SSD, delivering average-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring an ultra-high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores via Intel Xeon 8375C at 3.5 GHz
512.0 GB DDR4 RAM delivering 32.0 GB of memory per core
475 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-intensive data processing
